pain_point_label: Adhoc Reporting Request Backlog & Slow Turnaround

matches.match_rationale: The core bottleneck is 2-4 hours of manual SQL querying and Excel work per request. AI code generation can convert natural language questions ('How many students from Visayas in nursing?') directly into SQL or Python/Excel formulas, eliminating the manual query-writing step. This directly addresses the throughput problem for the 1-2 person data team.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A lightweight tool where the data team pastes a request and gets a ready-to-run SQL query against their SIS/MIS schema, cutting turnaround from hours to minutes without replacing the analyst entirely.

matches.match_rationale: Leadership and department heads need to self-serve answers to factual questions about institutional data without queuing requests to the data team. A RAG system grounded in pre-computed reports, data exports, and dashboards allows leaders to ask natural language questions and receive cited, accurate answers instantly. This directly offloads the high-volume, low-complexity tier of ad-hoc requests that dominate the backlog.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An internal Q&A interface where any administrator types a question like 'Faculty utilization rate by campus this semester?' and gets an answer synthesized from the latest exported reports and data summaries — no data team involvement required.

matches.match_rationale: The pain is not just one-off query generation but an ongoing dialogue between leadership and institutional data — leaders want to 'test hypotheses' and 'respond to market opportunities,' which implies iterative follow-up questions. A conversational agent maintains context across turns, allowing a department head to refine a data question ('Now break that down by campus', 'Compare to last year') without re-queuing each sub-question.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A conversational data assistant embedded in the school's intranet or messaging tool (Slack/Teams) that lets any administrator have a multi-turn data conversation, with the agent generating and executing queries against a read-only analytics database and returning formatted results.

matches.match_rationale: Recurring ad-hoc reports follow predictable patterns (compliance submissions, HR headcounts, enrollment breakdowns) that could be automated end-to-end: receive request via email/form, parse intent, query the SIS, format results, and route back to requester. This addresses the queue growth problem by automating the full workflow for the majority of requests, not just the SQL step.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An intake-to-delivery automation pipeline: department heads submit report requests via a form, the agent classifies the request, runs the appropriate query template, assembles an Excel/PDF output, and emails it back — handling the 70% of requests that are routine without data team involvement.
